title: AGENTS.md project guide
description: Data contract for the agents-md format: a single Markdown document (AGENTS.md, the open agents.md / Claude Code memory-file convention) that tells coding agents how to work on a project — setup/build/test commands, code style, conventions, testing, security, and PR rules. The template renders the Markdown as a human-friendly, themeable preview (NOT raw text) with an auto-generated heading outline; the exact file text is preserved in document.source for download/round-trip. The sample's #report-data is a valid instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared archive chrome. Brand-neutral: keep {{ORG}} / {{PROJECT}} / {{AUTHOR}} placeholders unless real values are supplied. meta.title and meta.subtitle drive the archive card title/abstract; meta.theme sets the initial brand colour.

project.description: Project / repository name the guide is for, e.g. 'acme-web' or {{PROJECT}}.

reportType.description: Eyebrow label shown above the title, e.g. 'Agent guide'. Defaults to 'AGENTS.md' when omitted.

title.description: Document title shown in the masthead H1, e.g. 'acme-web — agent guide'.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line summary shown under the title and used as the archive abstract.

date.description: ISO date the guide was generated (YYYY-MM-DD).

author.description: Author / generator shown in the footer or {{AUTHOR}} placeholder.

theme.description: Initial brand theme applied as <html data-theme>. Default for this format is 'slate'.

document.description: The Markdown document itself. The template renders document.source as styled HTML; the heading outline (TOC) is derived from its headings automatically.

filename.description: The file name this document would be saved as in the repo. Conventionally 'AGENTS.md'. Must end in .md.

source.description: The COMPLETE Markdown text of AGENTS.md (CommonMark / GitHub-flavoured: headings, lists, fenced code, tables, blockquotes, links, bold/italic). This is the canonical, downloadable artifact and the only content source for the rendered preview. Base it on the agents.md open standard / Claude Code CLAUDE.md conventions: project overview, setup/build/test commands, code style, testing, security, and commit/PR rules.

summary.description: Optional one-line description of what the project is, surfaced near the top of the preview.

meta.title: acme-web — agent guide
meta.subtitle: How coding agents should set up, build, test, and contribute to the acme-web monorepo.
meta.author: {{AUTHOR}}

document.summary: acme-web is a TypeScript pnpm monorepo: a Next.js app (apps/web) and shared packages (packages/*).
document.source:
# AGENTS.md

Guidance for coding agents working on **acme-web**. Human contributors should read `README.md`; this file holds the build, test, and convention details an agent needs.

## Project overview

- **Stack:** TypeScript, Next.js (App Router), PostgreSQL via Prisma.
- **Layout:** pnpm workspace — `apps/web` (the app), `packages/ui` (component library), `packages/config` (shared lint/tsconfig).
- **Entry point:** `apps/web/src/app`.

## Setup commands

- Install deps: `pnpm install`
- Start dev server: `pnpm dev`
- Build: `pnpm build`
- Run tests: `pnpm test`
- Lint + typecheck: `pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck`

## Code style

- TypeScript **strict** mode; no `any` without a `// why:` comment.
- Single quotes, no semicolons, 2-space indent (enforced by Prettier — run `pnpm format`).
- Prefer functional components and hooks; colocate a component's styles and tests beside it.
- Name files `kebab-case.ts`; export one component per file.

## Testing

- Framework: Vitest + Testing Library.
- Write the failing test first, then make it pass.
- A change is not done until `pnpm test` is green and coverage on touched files does not drop.

## Security

- Never commit secrets; read config from environment variables.
- Validate all external input at the route boundary with `zod`.
- Server-only code stays under `src/server/**` and is never imported by client components.

## Commit & PR rules

1. Branch from `main`; keep each PR focused on one change.
2. Conventional commit messages (`feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:` …).
3. PRs must pass CI (lint, typecheck, test) before review.

> **Note:** nested `AGENTS.md` files override this one for their subtree — agents read the nearest file first.
